description:
This is a blocking UX bug in the `packages/pi-beadwork-extension` dashboard flow, not follow-on polish.

Problem:
- `/bw` currently opens as a centered text overlay without a real modal surface
- background transcript content visually bleeds through the overlay
- Issues/Workers/clarify overlays are rendered as wrapped text blobs rather than guided TUI layouts
- navigation/redraw churn is visually disruptive and reads as full-screen flicker
- the result does not meet the goal of the epic, which was an actionable beadwork dashboard with a usable TUI surface

Scope:
- rebuild the dashboard and related beadwork overlays using real pi TUI surface/layout primitives rather than plain wrapped text lines
- provide explicit modal chrome/surface separation (frame, padding, visual hierarchy, background treatment)
- turn the Issues tab into a guided navigable surface rather than a text dump
- make Workers and delegate/run clarify overlays visually consistent with the dashboard
- reduce visible redraw/flicker during navigation and detail refreshes
- stay within the existing single-epic dashboard scope; do not expand into deferred multi-epic orchestration work

Primary file lane:
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tui/dashboard.ts`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tui/issue-explorer.ts`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tui/worker-manager.ts`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tui/delegate-clarify.ts`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tui/run-clarify.ts`
- related unit/integration tests under `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__`

Acceptance criteria:
- `/bw` renders as a clearly distinct modal surface with visible containment from the transcript behind it
- modal/background content no longer visually blends together during normal use
- the Issues tab presents a guided navigable list/detail layout instead of a borderless wrapped-text block
- Workers and both clarify modals use the same visual system and are readable without relying on transcript separation
- navigation does not produce visually disruptive redraw behavior during normal movement/drill-in/detail refresh
- regression tests cover the new component structure and key navigation/render behavior
- `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`

comments.text: Manual progress update: rebuilt the dashboard, issue explorer, worker manager, and delegate/run clarify overlays around a shared framed-surface renderer with stable modal chrome/background fill. The Issues and Workers views now render as structured list/detail surfaces instead of raw wrapped text blobs, and the clarify modals use the same visual system. Local package gates are green: `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`, `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`, and `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`. Not landed yet; working tree still contains the implementation + updated regression tests.

User review feedback on current framed-surface pass:

1. `… N more lines` is currently hiding content without an in-panel paging/scroll affordance; this needs to be fixed rather than treated as acceptable overflow.
2. Workers footer copy like `/bw:workers opens ...` is clutter when already on that page.
3. Footer verbosity across tabs needs cleanup; keep only high-value contextual controls.
4. The Actions tab is not useful in its current form because it duplicates footer/command palette material.
5. Visual hierarchy is still weak: top status blob is too tall, sections have near-identical visual weight, spacing/color do not guide the eye, and nested issue drill-in still reads like a paragraph dump.
6. Workers tab is still missing direct in-view action shortcuts/selection flow for cancel/land/cleanup.

Agreed next pass for wx1.7:
- replace fixed-height clipping/`… N more lines` with real navigable list/detail panes or otherwise ensure hidden content is reachable
- compress dashboard header into a smaller status strip so the main content area gets more space
- remove or substantially repurpose the Actions tab (default assumption: remove)
- simplify per-tab footers to concise contextual hints only
- increase hierarchy in Issues/Workers/detail layouts (stronger section separation, tighter metadata summaries, less paragraph formatting)
- add direct worker actions from the Workers tab with visible enabled/blocked state and shortcuts

title: Rebuild beadwork overlays as real pi TUI surfaces instead of transparent text dumps
